void_doctrine.principle: A judgment obtained through fraud is void ab initio and may be challenged at any time

mcr_authority.rule: Independent action for fraud on the court — NO time limit

case_law.rule: Fraud upon the court vitiates entire proceeding

case_law.cite: Res-Care Inc v Roto-Die Inc 284 Mich App 84 (2009)
case_law.rule: No time limit to void a void judgment
Orders based on fraud are void and unenforceable
Court has inherent power to set aside void judgments
Fraud in obtaining a judgment makes it void

fruit_doctrine.principle: Evidence and orders derived from the initial fraud are themselves tainted and void
fruit_doctrine.application: Because PPO 2023-5907-PP was obtained through fraud: (1) All custody restrictions based on PPO findings are void, (2) All parenting time suspensions relying on PPO are void, (3) The August 2025 ex parte order is void (built on prior void orders), (4) All FOC recommendations tainted by void PPO are invalid

relief_available: Void all custody orders derived from PPO findings
Award damages for wrongful deprivation (268 days)
Sanctions against Emily for fraud upon the court

filing_vehicles.MCR 2.612 Motion: File in 14th Circuit — void orders in state court
filing_vehicles.Federal 1983: File in USDC WDMI — bypass state court entirely
filing_vehicles.COA Appeal: COA 366810 — appellate review of void orders
filing_vehicles.MSC Superintending Control: Ask MSC to void all orders as extraordinary relief

chain.event: PPO granted based on false allegations
Custody restrictions imposed citing PPO findings
FOC recommendations based on PPO history
August 2025 ex parte order suspending parenting time
